text:
Market today: The mood is cautiously optimistic for tech, with chip stocks specifically bouncing back strongly. This positive momentum in semiconductors is the real driver, directly impacting your heavily concentrated book.

Your holdings: SOXL is a major concern at -37.76% and flagged "SELL," amplified by its leveraged nature and your margin. SPAL, a new leveraged position, is also down significantly at -48.45%. QQQI is flagged "TRIM" at -2.09%. Remember margin amplifies these drops.

Watch: Keep a close eye on the continued strength in chip names. Critically, monitor the stops on your leveraged SOXL and SPAL positions.

Bottom line: Focus on aggressive risk management and stop-loss adherence, especially on your leveraged tech/chip positions, given your concentration and margin use.
plays: Consider exiting SOXL given its -37.76% loss and leveraged decay; respect its stop at 200.
Review SPAL's -48.45% loss and its stop at 21.38; leveraged ETFs demand strict risk management.
If the chip bounce continues, NVDA shows a scanner long setup at 206.47-206.24 with a stop at 205.57.
